structures.name: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION
structures.description: Defines program identity and metadata including the program name.

structures.name: ENVIRONMENT DIVISION
structures.description: Specifies runtime environment and file control definitions for flat and indexed files.

structures.name: FILE-CONTROL
structures.description: Declares external file assignments, organizations, access modes, record keys, and file status fields.

structures.name: DATA DIVISION
structures.description: Defines file records and working storage used throughout the program.

structures.name: FILE SECTION
structures.description: Declares file descriptors for the load file and indexed customer file using COPY books.

structures.name: WORKING-STORAGE SECTION
structures.description: Holds file status codes, record counters, and end-of-job display messages.

structures.name: 0000-Mainline
structures.description: Top-level execution flow of the program. Controls overall job sequencing by invoking initialization, processing, and termination paragraphs.

structures.name: 1000-Begin-Job
structures.description: Initializes program execution by opening input and output files and performing the first read of the load file.

structures.name: 2000-Process
structures.description: Main processing loop. Continues inserting records into the indexed file and reading the next input record until end-of-file is reached.

structures.name: 2100-Insert-CUSTFile
structures.description: Moves the current load file record into the indexed file record structure and triggers the write operation.

structures.name: 3000-End-Job
structures.description: Handles normal end-of-job processing by displaying summary statistics and closing all open files.

structures.name: 5000-Read-LOADFile
structures.description: Reads the next record from the load file, updates record counters, sets end-of-file condition, and performs error handling for read failures.

structures.name: 6000-Write-CUSTFile
structures.description: Writes a customer record to the indexed file, increments insert counters on success, and performs controlled termination on write errors.
